county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1843; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Barbour County Clerk (Philippi). Formed 1843 from Harrison, Lewis, and Randolph -- check parent counties for earlier records. Philippi was site of the first organized land battle of the Civil War (June 3, 1861). Covered bridges are a notable county landmark. Barbour County Historical Society has local materials. Pre-1863 records also at LVA Richmond. FamilySearch has microfilmed deed and marriage records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1772; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Eastern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Berkeley County Clerk (Martinsburg). One of WV's oldest counties, formed 1772 from Frederick (VA). Eastern Panhandle county with strong German and Scots-Irish settlement. Martinsburg was a major B&O Railroad hub -- railroad employment records at B&O Railroad Museum, Baltimore. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Significant enslaved population documented in tax lists. Berkeley County Historical Society and Martinsburg-Berkeley County Public Library have local materials. Belle Boyd (Confederate spy) connections documented.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1847; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Boone County Clerk (Madison). Southern coal county formed 1847 from Kanawha, Cabell, and Logan. Coal mining began in earnest in the 1880s -- company records are essential for tracing families. Mine Wars history (Paint Creek-Cabin Creek strike 1912-1913). Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Check parent counties for earlier records. Small population before coal boom means fewer pre-1880 records. Coal Heritage Trail resources document mining community life.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.record_losses: 1897 courthouse fire destroyed many early records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1836; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Braxton County Clerk (Sutton). BURNED COUNTY -- 1897 courthouse fire destroyed many early records. Formed 1836 from Kanawha, Lewis, and Nicholas. Pre-1836 records in parent counties; pre-1863 records at LVA. Sutton Lake area -- Army Corps of Engineers relocation records for displaced families. Braxton County Historical Society has reconstructed records. Timber industry significant in late 19th century. FamilySearch microfilm may have pre-fire copies of some records. Tax lists at WV Archives provide substitute documentation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1797; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Northern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Brooke County Clerk (Wellsburg). Northern Panhandle county formed 1797 from Ohio County. One of WV's smallest counties by area. Strong industrial heritage -- steel, glass, and pottery manufacturing. Wellsburg was an early Ohio River settlement. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Brooke County Historical Society. Check Ohio County for pre-1797 records. Good surviving records. Cross-reference with Ohio and Pennsylvania records due to Panhandle proximity.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1809; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Cabell County Clerk (Huntington). Formed 1809 from Kanawha. Huntington established 1871 as C&O Railroad terminus -- railroad records essential for post-1870 research. Marshall University Special Collections has extensive regional materials including oral histories and Appalachian collections. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Cabell County was the commercial center of southwestern WV. Significant enslaved population in antebellum period. Ohio River trade and steamboat records at Marshall University.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1856; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Calhoun County Clerk (Grantsville). One of WV's least populous counties, formed 1856 from Gilmer. Small, rural county -- families easier to trace due to small population. Check Gilmer for pre-1856 records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Oil and gas development from the 1860s. Calhoun County Historical and Genealogical Society has local materials. FamilySearch has microfilmed records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.record_losses: 1873 courthouse fire destroyed most early records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1858; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Clay County Clerk (Clay). BURNED COUNTY -- 1873 courthouse fire destroyed most early records. Formed 1858 from Braxton and Nicholas. Check parent counties for pre-1858 records; pre-1863 records at LVA. Very rural, mountainous county. Clay County Historical Society. Substitute sources include tax lists at WV Archives, state copies of land grants, and census records. Timber and salt industries in 19th century. Elk River communities documented in oral histories.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1845; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Doddridge County Clerk (West Union). Formed 1845 from Harrison, Tyler, Ritchie, and Lewis. Named for Philip Doddridge, Virginia legislator. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Oil and gas industry from the 1860s -- early petroleum development records. Doddridge County Historical Society. Check four parent counties for earlier records. Good surviving records from formation. Small population makes family tracing more manageable.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1831; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Fayette County Clerk (Fayetteville). Large county formed 1831 from Kanawha, Greenbrier, Nicholas, and Logan. Major coal mining county -- New River Gorge area was center of coal industry from 1870s. New River Gorge National Park and Preserve has historical resources. Hawks Nest Tunnel disaster (1930s) documented in WVU archives. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Fayette County Historical Society. Civil War battle sites at Carnifex Ferry and Fayetteville. Check parent counties for earlier records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA
